name: trace-debugging
context: Debug Playwright failures with traces, trace viewer, screenshots, videos, HTML reports, PWDEBUG, headed mode, and inspector.
info: Playwright traces record action timelines, DOM snapshots, network activity, console logs, screenshots, and test source context. This skill covers enabling traces in configuration, opening trace.zip artifacts, collecting screenshots and videos on failure, using HTML reports, running headed or debug mode, and interpreting common failure patterns. Agents use it when a browser test flakes, a locator cannot act, a screenshot is wrong, a request fails, or CI behavior differs from local runs.
body:
# Trace Debugging

Use traces first when a browser test fails. They show what Playwright saw and did at each step.

```typescript
use: {
  trace: 'on-first-retry',
  screenshot: 'only-on-failure',
  video: 'retain-on-failure'
}
```

```bash
npx playwright test --trace on
nodes/playwright/scripts/open-trace.sh test-results/login/trace.zip
PWDEBUG=1 npx playwright test tests/login.spec.ts
npx playwright test --headed tests/login.spec.ts
npx playwright show-report
```

Trace viewer is more reliable than guessing from logs because it includes DOM snapshots, actions, console output, and network events.

sections.title: Trace policy
sections.body: Use `trace: 'on-first-retry'` as the normal CI default: passing tests do not produce heavy traces, but flaky failures get enough evidence on retry. Use `trace: 'retain-on-failure'` when retries are disabled. Use `--trace on` for a focused local investigation. Avoid `trace: 'on'` across a huge suite unless storage costs are acceptable.

sections.title: Trace viewer
sections.body: Open a trace with `npx playwright show-trace trace.zip`. Inspect the action timeline, before/after DOM snapshots, locator resolution, screenshots, network calls, console output, and source line. If a click failed, check whether the locator matched multiple elements, the element was covered, disabled, detached, or navigation occurred earlier than expected.

sections.title: Debug modes
sections.body: Use `--headed` to watch a browser, `--debug` for Playwright Inspector, and `PWDEBUG=1` for step-by-step debugging. Use `page.pause()` only during local investigation and remove it before commit. Combine with `--grep` or a test file path to keep the run small.

sections.title: CI artifacts
sections.body: Upload `playwright-report/`, `test-results/`, screenshots, videos, and traces on failure. Keep retention finite. Review artifacts from authenticated pages before sharing outside the project because traces can include URLs, DOM text, request metadata, screenshots, and localStorage-derived state.

links.context: Screenshots complement traces for visual evidence

related.note: CI setup determines whether traces and reports are retained.

PWDEBUG.description: Enable Playwright Inspector when set to 1.
